race.name: IngaLåmi
race.tagline: Thirty kilometers in the tracks of Queen Inga — Norway's largest women-only ski race, a celebration of strength, solidarity, and Scandinavian winter from Birkebeineren Ski Stadium.

vitals.aid_stations: 3-4 stations with sports drink, fruit, warm beverages

climate.description: Lillehammer has one of the most reliable winter climates in Norway. The inland location delivers cold, dry conditions with consistent snow cover from November through April. Race day temperatures typically range from -15°C to -2°C.
challenges: Cold inland temperatures can drop below -20°C
Wax selection critical in variable Scandinavian conditions
Wind exposure on open sections near Lillehammer
Firm, fast tracks in extreme cold demand strong technique

course.primary: Classic-technique course starting from the legendary Birkebeineren Ski Stadium in Lillehammer, traversing the forested hills of Innlandet. The terrain is rolling with some meaningful climbs, winding through Norway's quintessential birch and pine forest landscape.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through Norwegian forest and hill terrain
course.grooming: World-class Norwegian standard — the same infrastructure that supports Birkebeinerrennet
features: Named after medieval Queen Inga, mother of King Håkon
Starts from the iconic Birkebeineren Ski Stadium (1994 Olympics)
Classic-only format honors Norwegian skiing tradition
Part of the Birkebeiner festival week of events
5,000+ women skiing together — powerful community atmosphere

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A massive, well-organized women-only event with 5,000+ participants. World-class infrastructure inherited from Lillehammer's Olympic and Birkebeiner legacy. Exceptional community atmosphere. The historical connection to Queen Inga adds meaning.

history.summary: IngaLåmi was founded in 2006 as Norway's answer to the growing demand for a major women-only ski event. Named after Queen Inga of Varteig, the 13th-century mother of King Håkon Håkonsson whose story is central to the Birkebeiner legend, the race honors women's role in Norwegian skiing history. Starting from the Birkebeineren Ski Stadium — the venue for the 1994 Winter Olympics biathlon and cross-country events — IngaLåmi has grown to become the largest women-only ski race in the world, with over 5,000 participants.
notable_facts: Named after Queen Inga of Varteig, mother of the Birkebeiner prince Håkon Håkonsson
Largest women-only ski race in Norway and one of the largest in the world
Uses the same world-class infrastructure as the Birkebeinerrennet
Birkebeineren Ski Stadium hosted the 1994 Lillehammer Winter Olympics
The race has grown from a few hundred to over 5,000 participants in under 20 years
Part of the Birkebeiner festival week alongside the main Birkebeinerrennet
